ci_support_linux_64_python3.12.____cpyth:
c_compiler:
- gcc
c_compiler_version:
- '14'
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
eclib:
- '20250627'
expat:
- '2'
fortran_compiler:
- gfortran
fortran_compiler_version:
- '14'
glpk:
- '5.0'
gmp:
- '6'
gsl:
- '2.7'
libblas:
- 3.9.* *netlib
libboost_headers:
- '1.88'
libcblas:
- 3.9.* *netlib
libflint:
- '3.3'
liblapack:
- 3.9.* *netlib
liblapacke:
- 3.9.* *netlib
libpng:
- '1.6'
mpfr:
- '4'
ntl:
- 11.6.0
numpy:
- '2'
pari:
- 2.17.* *_pthread
pin_run_as_build:
  python:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
python:
- 3.12.* *_cpython
readline:
- '8'
target_platform:
- linux-64
zip_keys:
- - c_compiler_version
  - cxx_compiler_version
  - fortran_compiler_version
zlib:
- '1'

about.description:
SageMath is a free open-source mathematics software system. It builds on top of many existing open-source packages.
On conda-forge it is split into several related packages:
* sagelib — the Python package sage with minimal dependencies to be able to `import sage.all`
* sage — the standard distribution of SageMath, i.e., ``sagelib`` with all standard dependencies
* sagemath-* — optional parts of SageMath that are not automatically installed by `sage`

build.script: echo "This is a meta-package that just pulls in dependencies. It packages no files of its own."

tests.script:
set -x
ln -s $PREFIX local

export SAGE_LOCAL=$PWD/local
export SAGE_ROOT=$PWD
ls $SAGE_ROOT
ls $SAGE_LOCAL
echo $SAGE_ROOT > "$SAGE_LOCAL/lib/sage-current-location.txt"

export SAGE_NUM_THREADS=$CPU_COUNT

set +e
python -m sage.doctest -p $CPU_COUNT --initial --all --optional=memlimit,sage | tee doctest.txt

cat doctest.txt | grep '^sage -t ' | grep '#' > doctest.summary.txt
echo `cat doctest.summary.txt | awk '{ print $9 }' | sort | grep -E '[0-9]+' | paste -sd+ - | bc` failing individual doctests
cat doctest.summary.txt | awk '{ print $9 }' | sort | grep -vE '[0-9]+' | sort | uniq -c

export SAGE_ROOT=$PWD
ls $SAGE_ROOT
ls $SAGE_LOCAL
echo $SAGE_ROOT > "$SAGE_LOCAL/lib/sage-current-location.txt"

export SAGE_NUM_THREADS=$CPU_COUNT

set +e
python -m sage.doctest -p $CPU_COUNT --initial --all --optional=memlimit,sage | tee doctest.txt

cat doctest.txt | grep '^sage -t ' | grep '#' > doctest.summary.txt
echo `cat doctest.summary.txt | awk '{ print $9 }' | sort | grep -E '[0-9]+' | paste -sd+ - | bc` failing individual doctests
cat doctest.summary.txt | awk '{ print $9 }' | sort | grep -vE '[0-9]+' | sort | uniq -c

name: sage

about.description:
SageMath is a free open-source mathematics software system. It builds on top of many existing open-source packages.
On conda-forge it is split into several related packages:
* sagelib — the Python package sage with minimal dependencies to be able to `import sage.all`
* sage — the standard distribution of SageMath, i.e., ``sagelib`` with all standard dependencies
* sagemath-* — optional parts of SageMath that are not automatically installed by `sage`

build.script: echo "This is a meta-package that just pulls in dependencies. It packages no files of its own."

tests.script:
set -x
ln -s $PREFIX local

export SAGE_LOCAL=$PWD/local
export SAGE_ROOT=$PWD
ls $SAGE_ROOT
ls $SAGE_LOCAL
echo $SAGE_ROOT > "$SAGE_LOCAL/lib/sage-current-location.txt"

export SAGE_NUM_THREADS=$CPU_COUNT

set +e
python -m sage.doctest -p $CPU_COUNT --initial --all --optional=memlimit,sage | tee doctest.txt

cat doctest.txt | grep '^sage -t ' | grep '#' > doctest.summary.txt
echo `cat doctest.summary.txt | awk '{ print $9 }' | sort | grep -E '[0-9]+' | paste -sd+ - | bc` failing individual doctests
cat doctest.summary.txt | awk '{ print $9 }' | sort | grep -vE '[0-9]+' | sort | uniq -c

set -x
ln -s $PREFIX local

export SAGE_LOCAL=$PWD/local
export SAGE_ROOT=$PWD
ls $SAGE_ROOT
ls $SAGE_LOCAL
echo $SAGE_ROOT > "$SAGE_LOCAL/lib/sage-current-location.txt"

export SAGE_NUM_THREADS=$CPU_COUNT

set +e
python -m sage.doctest -p $CPU_COUNT --initial --all --optional=memlimit,sage | tee doctest.txt

cat doctest.txt | grep '^sage -t ' | grep '#' > doctest.summary.txt
echo `cat doctest.summary.txt | awk '{ print $9 }' | sort | grep -E '[0-9]+' | paste -sd+ - | bc` failing individual doctests
cat doctest.summary.txt | awk '{ print $9 }' | sort | grep -vE '[0-9]+' | sort | uniq -c

set -x
ln -s $PREFIX local

export SAGE_LOCAL=$PWD/local
export SAGE_ROOT=$PWD
ls $SAGE_ROOT
ls $SAGE_LOCAL
echo $SAGE_ROOT > "$SAGE_LOCAL/lib/sage-current-location.txt"

export SAGE_NUM_THREADS=$CPU_COUNT

set +e

cat doctest.txt | grep '^sage -t ' | grep '#' > doctest.summary.txt
echo `cat doctest.summary.txt | awk '{ print $9 }' | sort | grep -E '[0-9]+' | paste -sd+ - | bc` failing individual doctests
cat doctest.summary.txt | awk '{ print $9 }' | sort | grep -vE '[0-9]+' | sort | uniq -c

raw_meta_yaml:
schema_version: 1

context:
  version: "10.8"
  name: sage

recipe:
  name: ${{ name }}
  version: "${{ version }}"

source:
  url: https://github.com/sagemath/sage/archive/${{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 496f90f77b569cb3c93610219d92d4abd390caf909e11f4f01d0d37d86017c8e
  patches:
    # See https://github.com/sagemath/sage/issues/42002
    - 0001-Allow-SageMath-to-start-when-neither-HOME-nor-DOT_SA.patch
    - 0001-Use-standard-.sage-implementation-in-doctests.patch
    - 0001-Teach-atomic_write-to-create-the-containing-director.patch
    # See https://github.com/sagemath/sage/pull/42005
    - 0002-Do-not-invoke-GAP-when-cross-compiling.patch
    # See https://github.com/sagemath/sage/pull/42006
    - 0003-Do-not-require-ecl-maxima-to-be-executable-when-cros.patch

build:
  number: 1
  skip: win or match(python, "<3.12") or match(python, ">3.14")

outputs:
  - package:
      name: sagelib
    requirements:
      build:
        - libtool
        - automake
        - m4
        - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
        - ${{ compiler('c') }}
        - ${{ stdlib("c") }}
        - ${{ compiler('fortran') }}
        - make
        - pkg-config
        - gettext
        - ninja
        - jinja2        3.1.*
        - if: build_platform != target_platform
          then: python
        - if: build_platform != target_platform
          then: cross-python_${{ target_platform }}
        - if: build_platform != target_platform
          then: cython        3.1.*
        - cysignals
        - memory-allocator
        - numpy
        - cypari2       >=2.2
        - gmpy2
        - numpy
      host:
        - pip
        - meson-python
        - libblas
        - libcblas
        - liblapack
        - liblapacke
        - libboost-headers
        - cliquer       1.23.*
        - cypari2       2.2.*
        - cysignals     1.12.*
        - cython        3.1.*
        - ecl           >=24.5.10,<25
        - eclib
        - ecm           7.*
        - expat
        - fflas-ffpack  2.5.*
        - future
        - gap-core      4.14.0.*
        - giac          1.9.*
        - givaro        4.2.*
        - glpk
        - gmp
        - gmpy2         >=2.2.1,<3
        - gsl
        - iml           1.0.*
        - jinja2        3.1.*
        - jupyter_core  >=5.8.1,<6
        - lcalc         2.1.*
        - libbraiding   1.3.1.*
        - libbrial      1.2.*
        - libflint
        - libgd         2.3.*
        - libhomfly     1.03.*
        - libpng
        - linbox        >=1.7.1
        - lrcalc        2.1.*
        - m4ri
        - m4rie
        - maxima        5.47.0.*
        - memory-allocator
        - mpc           1.*
        - mpfi          1.5.*
        - mpfr
        - ntl
        - numpy
        - pari
        - pkg-config
        - pkgconfig
        - planarity     4.*
        - ppl           1.2.*
        - pplpy         0.8.*
        - python
        - readline
        - rw            0.9.*
        - singular      ==4.4.1.p5
        - six
        - symmetrica    3.1.*
        - zlib
        # sage runtime deps
        - ipython       >=8.18.1,<9
        - pexpect       >=4.9.0,<5
        # for the symlinks
        - threejs-sage
        - notebook
        - jmol

        # sagelib needs bliss at build time but not at runtime, see sagemath-bliss
        - bliss >=0.77
        # sagelib needs sirocco at build time but not at runtime, see sagemath-sirocco
        - sirocco      >=2.1.1,<3

      # These dependencies are enough to `import sage` and start `sage` but lots of
      # things do not work. To get all the standard dependencies of Sage, install
      # the "sage" package (which requires this "sagelib" package.)
      # By now we could probably drop this separation and create one feedstock
      # with possibly multiple outputs.
      # However, a more minimal distribution of SageMath, such as this sagelib,
      # is often useful. Until there is some notion of a sagemath-core we
      # should probably keep sagelib around therefore.
      # See sagelib-feedstock/#169 for details.
      run:
        - ${{ pin_compatible("cypari2") }}
        - ${{ pin_compatible("cysignals") }}
        - ${{ pin_compatible("cython") }}
        - ${{ pin_compatible("ecl", upper_bound="x.x") }}
        - expat
        - future
        - ${{ pin_compatible("gap-core", upper_bound="x.x") }}
        - ${{ pin_compatible("giac", upper_bound="x.x") }}
        - ${{ pin_compatible("gmpy2", upper_bound="x.x") }}
        - ${{ pin_compatible("jinja2", upper_bound="x.x") }}
        - ${{ pin_compatible("jupyter_core") }}
        - memory-allocator
        - ${{ pin_compatible("pplpy", upper_bound="x.x") }}
        - primecountpy >=0.1.1,<0.2.0
        - python
        - ${{ pin_compatible("pexpect") }}
        - python-lrcalc
        - pytest
        # Explicitly pin singular since its run_exports is having issues, see https://github.com/conda-forge/singular-feedstock/issues/37
        - singular      ==4.4.1.p5
        - six
        - ${{ pin_compatible("threejs-sage") }}
        # Disable ipython 8.19.0 which breaks the interactive session, see https://github.com/sagemath/sage/issues/36975
        - ipython >=8.18.1,<9,!=8.19.0
        # sage.features.all cannot be loaded without packaging (used by the fricas feature)
        - packaging
      ignore_run_exports:
        from_package:
          - ${{ compiler('fortran') }}
          # Ideally, we'd only build the glue code for sirocco but not install that glue glue code.
          # However, this is currently not really supported in the meson setup,
          # so we just install the glue code and depend on sirocco normally
          # (since sirocco has no problematic dependencies, this only increases
          # the size of sagelib a bit but should otherwise not be a problem.)
          # - sirocco
          # Same comments as for sirocco also apply to bliss.
          # - bliss
    tests:
      - python:
          imports:
            - sage.all
            # Verify that https://github.com/conda-forge/sage-feedstock/issues/135 has been resolved
            - sage.modular.multiple_zeta
          pip_check: false
      - script:
          # Verify that the sage executable works (note that the REPL is not functional without ipywidgets)
          - sage --version
          - sage -c 'print(1 + 1)'
      # Test that some minimal doctests pass with this incomplete version of SageMath
      - files:
          recipe:
            - doctest-sagelib.sage
        script:
          - python -m sage.doctest doctest-sagelib.sage
        requirements:
          run:
            # Sage's doctesting framework uses a pretty printing command from sympy
            - sympy
            # The SageMath REPL (which is invoked by the doctesting) uses ipywidgets
            - ipywidgets
            # Feature detection which is used by many parts of SageMath uses pip
            - pip
  # Currently, there is no real sagemath-bliss or sagemath-sirocco package
  # supported by SageMath upstream anymore so we actually include them in the
  # sagelib package.
  # We could roll our own package separation by just including the relevant .so
  # files in sagemath-bliss and sagemath-sirocco. However, without support for
  # staging outputs this is very tedious, see
  # https://github.com/conda/ceps/pull/102 and
  # https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-smithy/issues/2057.
  # In the meantime, we keep these packages around so workflows that use them
  # can be easily upgraded and because we think that we'll likely bring such
  # packages back to support optional dependencies better.
  - package:
      name: sagemath-bliss
    build:
      script: install-sagemath-bliss.sh
    requirements:
      run:
        - ${{ pin_subpackage('sagelib', exact=True) }}
    tests:
      - python:
          imports:
            - sage.graphs.bliss
          pip_check: false
  - package:
      name: sagemath-sirocco
    build:
      script: install-sagemath-sirocco.sh
    requirements:
      run:
        - ${{ pin_subpackage('sagelib', exact=True) }}
    tests:
      - python:
          imports:
            - sage.libs.sirocco
          pip_check: false
  - package:
      name: sage
    build:
      script:
        - echo "This is a meta-package that just pulls in dependencies. It packages no files of its own."
    requirements:
      run:
        - ${{ pin_subpackage('sagelib', exact=True) }}

        # We require all dependencies with at least that version that upstream uses
        # in their SPKGs. For packages that (seem to) use semantic versioning and
        # are at least 1.0.0, we limit the version to the next major release.
        - blas-devel
        - conway-polynomials >=0.10
        - cvxopt >=1.3.2,<2
        - cypari2 >=2.2.4,<3
        - flintqs >=1.0,<2
        - fplll >=5.5.0,<6
        - fpylll >=0.6.4
        - gap-defaults >=4.14.0
        - gfan >=0.6.2
        - ipykernel >=6.30.1,<7
        - ipywidgets >=8.1.1,<9
        - jmol >=14.29.52,<15
        - jupyter_client >=8.3.1,<9
        - matplotlib-base >=3.10.5,<4
        - maxima >=5.46.0,<6
        # mpmath 1.4.* leads to lots of assert type(man) == MPZ failures
        - mpmath >=1.3.0,<1.4
        - nbclient >=0.8.0,<0.9
        - nbconvert >=7.9.2,<8
        - nbformat >=5.9.2,<6
        - networkx >=3.5,<4
        - notebook >=7.1.1,<8
        - palp >=2.21,<3
        - pillow >=11.3.0,<12
        - pkg-config
        - pkgconfig
        - python >=3.9
        - pythran >=0.18.0,<0.19
        - pytz >=2025.2
        - rpy2 >=3.4.5,<4
        - rubiks >=20070912
        - sagemath-db-combinatorial-designs >=20140630
        - sagemath-db-elliptic-curves >=0.8.1
        - sagemath-db-graphs >=20210214
        - sagemath-db-polytopes >=20170220
        - sagetex >=3.6.1,<4
        - scipy >=1.15.2,<2
        - sphinx >=8.2.3,<9
        - sqlite >=3.36,<4
        - sympow >=2.023.6,<3
        - sympy >=1.12.1,<2
        - tachyon >=0.99b6,<1
        - widgetsnbextension >=4.0.9,<5
        - zn_poly

        # required for sage.misc.package.pip_installed_packages
        - pip
    tests:
      - python:
          imports:
            - sage.all
          pip_check: false
      - script:
          content: |
            sage -v
            sage -c 1+1
            echo exit | sage
      - files:
          recipe:
            - doctest-sage.sage
            - doctest-sagelib.sage
        script:
          - python -m sage.doctest doctest-sagelib.sage doctest-sage.sage
      - files:
          source:
            - src/sage/
        requirements:
          run:
            - bc
            - git
            - patch
        script: |
          set -x
          ln -s $PREFIX local

          export SAGE_LOCAL=$PWD/local
          export SAGE_ROOT=$PWD
          ls $SAGE_ROOT
          ls $SAGE_LOCAL
          echo $SAGE_ROOT > "$SAGE_LOCAL/lib/sage-current-location.txt"

          export SAGE_NUM_THREADS=$CPU_COUNT

          set +e
          python -m sage.doctest -p $CPU_COUNT --initial --all --optional=memlimit,sage | tee doctest.txt

          cat doctest.txt | grep '^sage -t ' | grep '#' > doctest.summary.txt
          echo `cat doctest.summary.txt | awk '{ print $9 }' | sort | grep -E '[0-9]+' | paste -sd+ - | bc` failing individual doctests
          cat doctest.summary.txt | awk '{ print $9 }' | sort | grep -vE '[0-9]+' | sort | uniq -c

about:
  license: GPL-2.0-or-later
  license_file: COPYING.txt
  summary: Open Source Mathematical Software
  description: |
     SageMath is a free open-source mathematics software system. It builds on top of many existing open-source packages.
     On conda-forge it is split into several related packages:
     * sagelib — the Python package sage with minimal dependencies to be able to `import sage.all`
     * sage — the standard distribution of SageMath, i.e., ``sagelib`` with all standard dependencies
     * sagemath-* — optional parts of SageMath that are not automatically installed by `sage`
  homepage: https://www.sagemath.org
  repository: https://github.com/sagemath/sage
  documentation: https://doc.sagemath.org

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - isuruf
    - saraedum
    - alexjbest
